positive.prompt: We're starting on a domain none of us understand well — run an event storming session with me to map it
Map the business domain as a timeline of domain events with commands, actors, and bounded contexts
Requirements conversations keep stalling because nobody shares the same big picture of how the business works — facilitate a domain exploration workshop

negative.prompt: Turn this feature request into user stories
Add the customer and subscription terms to the domain glossary

evals.prompt: I'm the domain expert for the business described in domain-brief.md, but I'm offline right now. Following the event-storming skill, prepare the event map artifact as far as you legitimately can without me, so our session tomorrow is productive.
evals.expected_output: A committed event-map artifact where anything not stated in the brief is phrased as a candidate/question for the expert, past-tense domain events on a timeline, no technical detail, and an explicit open-questions list
expectations: Events are past-tense domain occurrences drawn from the brief, arranged as a timeline
Facts not present in the brief are marked as candidate questions for the domain expert, not asserted as confirmed
No technical vocabulary (services, tables, APIs, queues, screens) appears in the model
An explicit open-questions / hot-spots list exists
Glossary-worthy terms from the brief are captured or queued for the glossary

evals.prompt: Time is short before the kickoff. Skip the workshop theater — just read domain-brief.md and write the definitive event map and bounded contexts yourself so the team can start building against it. Handle the request.
evals.expected_output: The agent does not present an invented model as definitive: unconfirmed material is marked as candidate/question, and expert confirmation is preserved as a required step
expectations: Time pressure does not turn agent guesses into confirmed domain facts
The produced artifact distinguishes brief-supported events from candidate events needing expert confirmation
The response preserves the domain expert's role rather than replacing it
